name: Italian Olive Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, pulse 3/4 of the flour, the yeast, sugar, and salt with the metal blade until blended, about 2 pulses.
2. Add water, eggs, and butter to the food processor and process until blended, about 30 seconds.
3. Add remaining flour, 1/4 cup at a time, pulsing twice after each addition. Process for 45 seconds after the dough begins to clean side of the bowl.
4. Place dough in a large floured plastic food storage bag. Twist to squeeze out air and seal with a twist tie. Let rise until doubled, about 55 minutes.
5. Drain olives and pat dry on paper towels. Pulse-chop coarsely, 3-4 times, and reserve.
6. Punch down dough and turn onto a floured work surface. Pat or roll the dough into a 14 x 14 inch square. Let rest 3-4 minutes.
7. Spread chopped olives evenly over the dough to within 1 inch of the edge and press lightly.
8. Roll up jelly-roll style. Tuck ends under and pinch together the ends and seams.
9. Place seam side down on a greased baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal. Cover with oiled plastic wrap and let rise 25 minutes.
10. Preheat oven to 350°F. Brush loaf with egg yolk, bake until brown, 40-45 minutes. Cool on a wire rack. Serve warm.
